Title: FLOURY ENDOSPERM 6 mutations enhance the sugary phenotype caused by the loss of ISOAMYLASE1 in barley

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CARBOHYDRATE-BINDING MODULE 48-containing protein; coiled-coil region; CBM48 domain
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant allele analysis (hvfo6-1/Franubet, hvfo6-2); allelism test; double-mutant analysis with hvisa1-3; RT-PCR; starch granule microscopy; TP-GFP plastid imaging; metabolite quantification
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing grain/endosperm; RT-PCR detected transcript in developing grains at 15 DAA
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth impairment; fewer tillers; reduced shoot fresh weight; shrunken grains when combined with hvisa1; reduced starch accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of HvFLO6 alters starch granule morphology and enhances the sugary/low-starch phenotype caused by HvISA1 loss, increasing phytoglycogen and sugars in the double mutant
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HvISA1 (by orthology/inference from rice FLO6-ISA1 interaction discussed in paper)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. hvfo6-2 is an allelic mutant of Franubet/hvfo6-1 and carries a premature stop codon. 2. HvFLO6 loss causes compound and enlarged simple starch granules in endosperm and compound granules in pollen. 3. In the hvisa1 background, hvfo6 strongly enhances reduction of starch and increases phytoglycogen and sugar accumulation, causing shrunken grains.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Starch is the major carbon/energy storage polymer in grain; soluble sugars contribute to the sugary grain phenotype and end-use quality
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; grain developmental stages 10-20 DAA and mature grain
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing grains/endosperm at 15 DAA; function also evident in pollen
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core non-enzymatic starch granule morphology factor. Double mutant with hvisa1 produced starch-rich and starch-less endosperm regions with balloon-like iodine pink structures consistent with phytoglycogen-rich amyloplast defects. Authors discuss additional functions beyond supporting ISA1.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant allele analysis (hvisa1-1/Risø17, hvisa1-2/Notch-2, hvisa1-3, hvisa1-4, hvisa1-5); sequencing; immunoblot; native-PAGE DBE activity staining; double-mutant analysis with hvfo6-2; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Protein accumulates in developing grains; assayed at 15 DAA
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced starch accumulation; sugary phenotype with elevated soluble sugars and phytoglycogen; when combined with hvfo6 causes severe shrunken grains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of HvISA1 reduces normal starch synthesis and promotes phytoglycogen and sugar accumulation; phenotype is enhanced by HvFLO6 loss
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. New barley hvisa1 alleles lacking HvISA1 protein were isolated. 2. HvISA1 loss causes compound starch granules, high phytoglycogen, altered α-glucan chain-length distribution, and elevated sugars. 3. Combining hvisa1 with hvfo6 further aggravates low-starch and sugary phenotypes.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Starch is the major reserve carbohydrate in grain; soluble sugars underlie the sugary phenotype
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; developing and mature grains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing grain/endosperm at 15 DAA; little apparent requirement in pollen based on phenotype
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzyme-encoding, the paper mainly studies mutant causality at whole-plant/metabolic level; however HvISA1 is clearly a starch debranching enzyme. haddii

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step not formally established; required for proper amylopectin crystallization and suppression of phytoglycogen accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced starch accumulation, shrunken grains in double mutant background, elevated soluble sugars/phytoglycogen
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HvFLO6 (genetic interaction in barley; physical interaction reported for rice orthologs as discussed)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to create sugary/high-soluble-carbohydrate grain traits, but severe starch loss and shrunken grain especially with HvFLO6 disruption are major cautions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoter/mutant alleles; no transgenic overexpression promoter used for focal gene
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream/post-branch trimming step in amylopectin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for proper storage polymer assembly; prevents diversion of glucan flux to phytoglycogen and supports starch accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Grain development (10-20 DAA; mature grain) under normal growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Starch; phytoglycogen; glucose; fructose; sucrose; maltose
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: HvISA1 protein accumulates in developing grains at 15 DAA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Debranched/trimmed amylopectin glucan structure enabling semicrystalline starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes α-1,6-glucosidic branch linkages in amylopectin/glycogen-like glucans to remove improperly positioned branches during starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch accumulation and suppresses phytoglycogen and soluble sugar accumulation in barley grain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing endosperm/grain; not evidently required for normal pollen starch morphology
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Isoamylase-type debranching enzyme; catalytic triad and dimerization domain
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Null alleles lacked HvISA1 protein/activity. Loss causes compound starch granules and altered α-glucan chain-length distribution with more short chains.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Branched α-glucans/amylopectin-like glucans containing α-1,6 linkages
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple new hvisa1 alleles were identified and shown to lack HvISA1 protein/activity. 2. hvISA1 loss caused compound starch granules, increased phytoglycogen, altered α-glucan chain distribution, and elevated grain sugars. 3. hvfo6 mutation strongly enhanced the low-starch/sugary phenotype of hvisa1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant sequencing; immunoblot; DBE activity staining; grain microscopy; phytoglycogen, starch and sugar quantification; genetic interaction/double mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage reserve carbohydrate affecting grain quality and carbon partitioning
